description: I often work with companies who have no concept of change management but know that their system doesn't work so well. And in some cases their customer demand software traceability. devZing SVN allows me to demonstrate this and make the software more compliant. I had some problems creating users at one time and contact tech support. They responded right away with a fix. I was pleased with the speed of their response because I needed to enroll my program right away.
author: David C.
pros: devZing makes it easy to create new SVN repositories to support my various projects. Originally, the repositories had a simple flat structure but have since been enhanced to include all the standard SVN folders. I use the repositories daily to not only save my revisions but also to transfer programs from development to production and vice versa. I use the SVN changelog daily also to keep track of my time. This is my revenue so I'm glad it works well.
cons: User management is a little clumsy. I'd like to see my user be a super user that always has access to all repositories. It isn't hard to add a user to a repository so this is not a big deal. Just seems like a feature that would enhance the experience a bit.

description: In the rare cases we have needed support , it has always been prompt and of excellent quality, with our issues resolved promptly. This, to me, is the best reason to choose devZing.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Bugzilla is an industry standard tool for tracking bugs and enhancements for software. We wanted to improve out bug tracking capability without incurring the overhead of internally hosting a sever for Bugzilla. devZing was the answer. Their hosted Bugzilla service is well maintained with great support and a really good price.
cons: While devZing keep Bugzilla current, they do not develop or control Bugzilla. It is an open source offering and as such is constrained by the resource that go into it and the priorities of the open source community that supports Bugzilla. There are features we'd like to see, such as the ability to edit or even delete a bug comment that are not withing devZing's control. What is in their control would be larger upload attachments sizes.

review_title: it was nice to use for a while but not for real projects
description: devZing is simple and doesn't have a lot of the features developers use today like advanced integration with git / github and the automation of ticket status.
author: Mohamed A.
pros: it was very simple to setup and use, and the UI is not as complex as other bug trackers like mantis.
cons: devZing is simple and doesn't have a lot of the features developers use today like advanced integration with git / github and the automation of ticket status.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: We changed to devZing for hosting Redmine. A very smooth transition with guidance as needed.
author: Craig M.
pros: We had several issues with a different host provider including the lack of support when needed. Moving to devZing was the best thing that we have done. devZing helped to make the transition seamless. Everything came up working perfectly. AND several of the issues that we had with the previous provider disappeared. This is significant. Telling customers that we had email issues with our issue tracker and could not send them notifications - because the provider was blacklisted and flagged as spam throughout the internet - was extremely embarrassing and inefficient. Now with devZing, that issue has gone away.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Great guys. The one time I had a question with customer service they were very prompt and helped me. Good honest service, definitely worth it.
cons: I think I forgot sometimes what the actual "limits" were on the plan I'd subscribed too and tried to upload too large of files. That was my bad tho.

review_title: Ease of use, fast customer service.
description: Hosted and managed bug tracking (bugzilla) made easy with quick support turn around.
author: Jeff M.
pros: Cloud based, low cost, fast customer service.
cons: UI is dated, bugzilla in itself can be quite complicated to tailor.

description: devZing is our primary provider of Subversion service.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It works! Don't remember any availability issue.
cons: Nothing in particular. The user interface is a kind of minimalistic.

description: We have been using devZing for over 5 years, the customer support is great and the price is affordable. At this point we dont have plan to change.
author: Ramon S.
pros: Easy to setup. Easy to manage. Great for a little testing company with several customers.
cons: Could be great to integrate it with others apps for project management.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It's simple. It works. Within 5 minutes you are up and running.
cons: Their site is not very pretty, but it's functional, so this doesn't really matter.

author: Ben L.
pros: DevZing provides a great, minimalist SVN hosting service. It is easy to use and the customer support is excellent.
cons: No complaints. I've used DevZing SVN hosting for years and never had any problems with it.

review_title: I often use this tool and has been using this for a couple of years, I like its web platform like
description: Overall Bugzilla has a proven track record of being a very good tool - esp for start-ups with less money and/or either a company or a new sub-department within the organization - where the management doesn't want to invest a lot of money.Overall Bugzilla has a proven track record of being a very good tool - esp for start-ups with less money and/or either a company or a new sub-department within the organization - where the management doesn't want to invest a lot of money.
author: Ciby A.
pros: Easy of installation. Browser compatible- with most of the varieties of standard ones. The basics are easy to use. Free and open source option is available, which is great for young tech start-ups.
cons: UI design not attractive. Customization difficult. I felt the UI needs some improvement - alongwith Usability. The other paid tools have good customizability - defining the workflows or even showing proper labels.

review_title: Great support. Their team is always ready to help.
author: Abhay M.
pros: The interface is very basic, but provides all the necessary features. Not much else to say regarding this.
cons: We have not experienced any downtime with them. Their support response time is very low. When we send an email request then we usually get a response back in a few hours.

description: I was working with a team of 15 developers on a project. We were using the project management and repository tools offered by devZing. The simplicity and easy of use is the best advantage of this service.
author: Stoyan C.
pros: Simple and reliable. I always can count on the great support even though I don't have to use it often. I love it.
cons: The biggest advantage, simplicity and easy of use, is the worst disadvantage at the same time. Does not offer advanced tools for Project Management and Repositories.

review_title: Nice service for the small to medium projects
author: Michael P.
pros: Price and maturity - it just works. Use for my archived personal things.
cons: Lack of features compared to GitHub. Hardly works for larger teams.

author: Brian H.
pros: Easy to setup and use. I was able to commit my source code quickly and easily
cons: Did not see a feature to backup the source code or download it.

description: stable and simple to use, love it
author: Sawan R.
pros: the product has been stable ever since I have purchased it. The support staff is very helpful.
cons: It could have more features, but may be keeping it simple is helpful

author: Jonathon B.
pros: Adds value to our line of business through cost savings and value added features.
cons: I have found no negative value from using this software.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It is a reliable, useful and good value product for money. I have been using it for 3/4 years or so, and it has never let me down. Besides, the support team is great, with quick and resolutive answer.
cons: Only the web GUI and ease to use may be improvable.

description: The function to export bug list has been very helpful for our quality department for reports.
author: Phoebe D.
pros: We have been using Bugzilla for years and it's been a pretty helpful tool in our software development. I am a system admin but also a test engineer. I was using Bugzilla to log the bugs. It's very user friendly, simple to use and straight forward. Also the customer service is awesome. We recently changed our business credit card and forgot to update in DevZing. They reactivated the service for us even when we didn't realize what the problem was. When they got in touch with me about the card, they helped updated our info very quickly and the system was back on in time. Thanks.
cons: The software is easy to use overall but the UI isn't the best in the field. I've used other bug tracking software before and bugzilla is the one that is more user friendly. But I think that brought this problem because it's easy to use so the UI could be less fancy. If the UI can be upgraded that would be better.

author: Mike F.
pros: The guy on the backend helping to customize as needed!
cons: Nothing in particular. Everything worked as expected.

description: We have had a good experience with devZing. Good pricing, good support. The few issues we have had with SSL keys and URL registration were addressed quickly.
author: Trevor Z.
pros: The hosting service is solid and support is quick and professional.
cons: I have no concerns about the hosting service. Bugzilla has a very basic user interface but it is what it is.

review_title: A great way to track tasks
description: Great support. Quick responses to questions and downtime issues.
author: Mike S.
pros: We were able to customize it for our needs. It was easy for our employees to learn and use this system.
cons: It's a bit old school. Not as pretty as newer services.

review_title: We are using devZing from couple of years and it is easy to use.
author: Prashant G.
pros: I am using this portal from more than 2 year and it is good experience to use this software. It has a great design and easy to use.
cons: Cons : What I feel recently about SVN integeration with bugZilla can be improved. If we can change status, hours and other fields of perticular bug from commit window of SVN using some IDE like VS, eclipse etc... then this would be great.

review_title: This was very easy to setup and without any need for support.
author: Rikard J.
pros: We only used Bugzilla and it was very simple and easy to distribute the tool and give access to users outside of our organization.
cons: Subscribing on changes generated e-mails lokking the it was sent from the administraters own email, people put filter to net get spammed. This resulted in that the administrators own emails was missed (Sent to trashcan)

review_title: Great SubVersion hosting product with outstanding customer support
author: David A.
pros: Very simple streamlined administrator interface for SubVersion hosting, fast servers, outstanding North America based customer support.
cons: Can't think of anything major. This is minor, but the list of repositories in the admin panel of my account at devzing.com is a bit too wide using Opera browser and therefore appears a bit wonky/outside the frame.
